groups.question_title: Which TWO problems are caused by water hyacinth?
groups.desc:
Questions 21 and 22
Choose TWO letters, A-E.

Questions 23-26
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.title: Where was water hyacinth originally from?
What is the primary cause of the decrease in nutrients from the soil?
When will the biological solution bring risks to the environment?
What does John say about the mechanical solution?

groups.question_title: What is the benefit of the cut-down water hyacinth to each of the following aspects?
groups.desc:
Questions 27-30
What is the benefit of the cut-down water hyacinth to each of the following aspects?
Choose FOUR answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-F, next to Questions 27-30.

collect_option.title: Benefits of the cut-down water hyacinth

list.title: A
list.content: can reduce the effect of global warming

list.title: B
list.content: can be used as an alternative energy resource

list.title: D
list.content: can produce materials for plants to grow in

list.title: E
list.content: can be good for human health

transcript.speaker: Arya
transcript.en: John, how is it going with your research on water hyacinth?

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: Well, I went on a field trip on my own and collected the primary data.

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: After I came back, I also referred to the published data.

transcript.speaker: Arya
transcript.en: So what do you know about this plant?

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: Water hyacinth grows in tropical countries.

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: It has beautiful purple-blue flowers, but everybody hates it.

transcript.speaker: Arya
transcript.en: Why is that?

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: Water hyacinth, as the world's worst invasive aquatic plant, has attracted significant attention.

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: Millions of these plants grow in rivers and lakes and have brought huge environmental and cultural problems to the local people.

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: Farmers cannot travel in boats on the water, and thus cannot fish in it.

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: In addition, heavy free-floating mats of water hyacinth are clogging the dam at the power station in West Africa.

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: Turbines of the power station are often shut down because of the intertwined leaves.

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: Dams often cease to function as a result, severely restricting the electricity generation.

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: Dense mats of water hyacinth also shade the surface and prevent algae growth, thus limiting the production of oxygen and threatening the survival of fish.

transcript.speaker: Arya
transcript.en: So where did water hyacinth originate from?

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: Water hyacinth is native to Amazon Basin in Latin America.

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: It has spread mainly to the tropics and subtropics throughout much of the world since the 1800s.

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: Later, it was introduced into Europe and Africa as an ornamental garden pond plant due to its beauty.

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: Flowers of this plant were being sold in local markets in African countries as a source of income for women.

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: Since the 1900s, this weed has infested lakes, rivers and creeks of the delta areas in West Africa.

transcript.speaker: Arya
transcript.en: I guess whoever introduced this plant had never expected the disastrous impact it would have on the local ecological environment.

transcript.speaker: Arya
transcript.en: What kind of habitat does water hyacinth thrive most in?

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: Well, it has been discovered that the nutrient-rich environment contributes significantly to the rapid proliferation of this weed, which obtains its nutrients directly from the water.

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: This is normally compounded with a decrease of nutrients along the river banks.

transcript.speaker: Arya
transcript.en: Why is that?

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: The main reason for soil nutrient loss is that local residents cut down trees.

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: Excessive deforestation accelerates soil erosion, which indirectly compounds this issue.

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: Without enough trees to retain nutrients in the soil, frequent rain might partly aggravate this by carrying some of the nutrients into the waterways.

transcript.speaker: Arya
transcript.en: Are there any effective measures to control or even eradicate this weed?

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: Yes, a number of approaches have been widely applied throughout the world.

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: Among them, biological control is the most widely favoured long-term control method by introducing a type of insect that feeds on water hyacinth.

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: So far, Nigeria has used this method for 6 months.

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: However, water hyacinth won't be removed immediately.

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: The side effects of this method on local ecology will remain unforeseen for years because it takes time for this external insect species to reach a density sufficient enough to affect ecological development.

transcript.speaker: Arya
transcript.en: Then what about other approaches?

transcript.speaker: Arya
transcript.en: Is there a quicker way to control it?

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: Well, the mechanical removal of water hyacinth is seen as the best short-term solution.

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: This option includes harvesting plants and in-site cutting.

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: This method can immediately open physical space for fish and boat traffic.

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: It is, however, inefficient for workers to process extensive areas, and costly to use large cutting and dredging equipment.

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: Ironically, it costs even more to dispose of this plant than to remove it.

transcript.speaker: Arya
transcript.en: Can we make use of this weed and turn it into something good?

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: Now scientists think that water hyacinth can be useful.

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: The plant itself, although more than 95% water, has a fibrous tissue and a high energy and protein content, and can be used for a variety of useful applications.

transcript.speaker: Arya
transcript.en: So what do people do with water hyacinth that is initially removed?

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: First, the cut-down water hyacinth is left to dry under the sun for a few days before being mixed with ash and animal manure.

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: The mixture can be used as alternative soil rather than fertiliser.

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: It can grow crops with increased yield.

transcript.speaker: Arya
transcript.en: That sounds interesting.

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: Mushroom farmers can benefit from water hyacinth as well.

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: Using the mixture mentioned earlier, mushrooms generally grow much faster so that farmers can harvest mushrooms sooner, which enables them to make money faster.

transcript.speaker: Arya
transcript.en: I see.

transcript.speaker: Arya
transcript.en: It can be used to grow oyster and straw mushrooms, right?

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: Yes.

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: In India, attempts have been made to grow these types of edible mushrooms organically utilizing water hyacinth.

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: These mushrooms provide sufficient nutritional values including minerals and high-quality protein which are essential to people's level of fitness.

transcript.speaker: Arya
transcript.en: Right.

transcript.speaker: Arya
transcript.en: Are there any other measures taken to utilise water hyacinth?

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: In South-east Asia, people feed cows using chopped water hyacinth mixed with other vegetables.

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: Then the waste from cows is treated to produce methane gas, which can be used as fuel for cooking, lighting or powering an engine.

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: And with global warming getting worse, developing alternative sources of energy will be more urgent than ever.

transcript.speaker: Arya
transcript.en: Maybe in the future people will love water hyacinth instead of hating it.

transcript.speaker: John
transcript.en: Yes, that's very likely if we can make full use of this weed.

21.analysis:
听前预测：提示词 problems。
题目解析：注意 water hyacinth 是全篇的主题词，难以用于定位。题干的条件限定为 problems，审题时可标画“↓”箭头，重点听负向态度信息。录音中从 worst 开始出现负向态度，随后听到了 problems to the local people，但没提及中毒，排除选项 D。选项 B 中的 Farmers 紧接着出现，提到了他们不能划船，因此无法捕鱼，故选项 B 正确。选项 C 中的 dam 出现，但未提及它的 structure 如何，而是说它常因为发电机涡轮叶片被水葫芦的叶子缠住而停止运转，从而严重限制了电能供给，选项 E 信息较为完整，更符合录音中的描述，优先选择 E；录音中的 electricity generation 对应选项 E 中的 Electricity production，severely restricting 则对应 is affected。选项 A 中的 Plants 在录音中被替换为 algae，fish 原词出现，不过同样未提及中毒。因此答案为 B & E。

22.analysis:
听前预测：提示词 problems。
题目解析：注意 water hyacinth 是全篇的主题词，难以用于定位。题干的条件限定为 problems，审题时可标画“↓”箭头，重点听负向态度信息。录音中从 worst 开始出现负向态度，随后听到了 problems to the local people，但没提及中毒，排除选项 D。选项 B 中的 Farmers 紧接着出现，提到了他们不能划船，因此无法捕鱼，故选项 B 正确。选项 C 中的 dam 出现，但未提及它的 structure 如何，而是说它常因为发电机涡轮叶片被水葫芦的叶子缠住而停止运转，从而严重限制了电能供给，选项 E 信息较为完整，更符合录音中的描述，优先选择 E；录音中的 electricity generation 对应选项 E 中的 Electricity production，severely restricting 则对应 is affected。选项 A 中的 Plants 在录音中被替换为 algae，fish 原词出现，不过同样未提及中毒。因此答案为 B & E。

23.analysis:
听前预测：提示词 Where、originally from。
题目解析：本题题干句式与录音原文基本一致，比较容易定位，答案出现得较快。be originally from 在录音中被替换为 be native to，其后的 Latin America 即为最初来源地。因此答案为 A。

24.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 nutrients、soil；提示词 primary cause、decrease。
题目解析：本题极具迷惑性。在 nutrients 出现后听到了“Why is that?”，提示接下来的是原因，需要集中注意力听。先是听到：“The main reason for soil nutrient loss is that local residents cut down trees.”其中 main reason 替换了题干中的 primary cause，soil nutrient loss 替换了 decrease in nutrients from the soil，故选项 C 正确。之后出现了干扰信息 soil erosion 和 frequent rain，分别对应选项 A 和 B，但 indirectly 和 partly 说明它们不符合题干限定 primary cause。因此答案为 C。

25.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 biological solution、environment；提示词 risks。
题目解析：题干的条件限定为 risks，审题时可标画“↓”箭头，重点听负向态度信息。选项 B 中的 6 months 和选项 A 中的 immediately 先后出现，不过都与 risks 无关。接着录音中出现了负向态度信息 side effects，符合题干目标信息方向，注意听随后的内容：“... of this method on local ecology will remain unforeseen for years ...”其中 this method 指的就是题干中的 biological solution，local ecology 对应题干中的 environment，remain ... for years 替换了选项 C 的 many years later。因此答案为 C。

26.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 mechanical solution；提示词 John。
题目解析：本题的三个选项均为负向信息，在听到定位词 mechanical solution 后，注意关注男生所表达的负向态度。录音中从 however 开始出现负向信息：“It is, however, inefficient for workers to process extensive areas, and costly to use large cutting and dredging equipment. Ironically, it costs even more to dispose of this plant than to remove it.”其中 inefficient 意为“低效的”，注意与选项 A 的 ineffective（无效的）进行区分。costly、costs even more 都表达了选项 C 的 expensive 含义。因此答案为 C。

27.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 dried water hyacinth。
题目解析：定位词 dried water hyacinth 在录音中被替换为 water hyacinth is left to dry，但比较容易识别。之后听到：“The mixture can be used as alternative soil rather than fertiliser. It can grow crops with increased yield.”其中 alternative soil 为选项 D 中 materials 的具体展开替换，crops 对应 plants。虽然选项 F 中的 fertiliser 有提及，但通过否定信号词 rather than 可排除。因此答案为 D。

28.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 mushroom farmers。
题目解析：定位词 mushroom farmers 在录音中原词重现，随后出现答案相关信息：“Using the mixture mentioned earlier, mushrooms generally grow much faster so that farmers can harvest mushrooms sooner, which enables them to make money faster.”其中 make money faster 替换了选项 C 中的 make quick profits。因此答案为 C。

29.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 oyster and straw mushrooms。
题目解析：定位词 oyster and straw mushrooms 原词重现于女生的提问中，随后男生进行了详细阐述。接着听到：“These mushrooms provide sufficient nutritional values including minerals and high-quality protein which are essential to people's level of fitness.”其中 nutritional values、minerals and high-quality protein 和 essential to people's level of fitness 与选项 E 中的 good for human health 对应。因此答案为 E。

30.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 cows。
题目解析：定位词 cows 同样原词重现，答案句随即出现：“Then the waste from cows is treated to produce methane gas, which can be used as fuel for cooking, lighting or powering an engine.”其中 methane gas 意为“沼气”，并提及这是一种燃料，它是选项 B 中的 alternative energy 的具体展开替换。虽然干扰选项 A 中的 global warming 在后一句中被提及，但录音中并未明确表示水葫芦可用于减少全球变暖，所以可排除。因此答案为 B。
